title: Internal Service Catalog And Readiness Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the internal service catalog and readiness capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Maintains an internal catalog of platform services and aggregates their readiness probes into a single tenant-scoped platform services readiness contract, so operators and tenant admins can see which composed services are ready. The capability is provider-backed and tenant-aware; it must keep its lifecycle/readiness model stable across extraction and preserve tenant isolation on what each tenant can observe.

stateModel.description: It owns no database schema of its own: service descriptors and readiness come from the catalog and from per-service readiness probes accessed through a readiness-probe port, with the capability holding a derived, tenant-scoped readiness record. Probe results are aggregated into discrete ready/degraded/blocked states per service, and behaviour changes update semantic artefacts rather than introducing owned tenant rows.

permissions.description: Catalog and readiness reads are scoped to the caller's tenant so a tenant sees only the services and readiness it is entitled to, with standard platform security controls applied. Because the readiness view can expose tenant-visible posture, isolation must be preserved and unscoped access fails closed rather than revealing other tenants' service state.

contracts.description: Exposes a tenant-scoped platform services readiness query backed by a provider readiness-probe port, so individual service health checks are swappable without changing the catalog contract. The aggregate response has a stable shape (per-service identity plus readiness state) and composes with the composed-provider readiness spine for end-to-end readiness reporting.

validation.description: Requests are validated for required tenant scope and well-formed service references before probes are invoked, and missing or invalid configuration fails closed instead of inventing default readiness. Aggregation invariants ensure a service with an unknown or failed probe is reported as not-ready rather than silently omitted.

errorModel.description: A failing or unavailable readiness probe yields a typed degraded/readiness failure for that service and never fabricates a ready state; mutation boundaries stay closed and the aggregate reflects the worst-case truth. Tenant data loss is possible only if backup/restore/export semantics are bypassed, so recovery preserves the tenant boundary and audit lineage.

auditModel.description: Readiness queries and catalog interactions are correlated to request and trace identifiers, and any state-affecting catalog action is recorded as an auditable event preserving tenant boundary and DSR/GDPR evidence. A missing tenant-isolation or audit signal on this surface is itself an alert condition.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is the capability's primary output: it aggregates per-service probes into a tenant-scoped posture that degrades explicitly when a probe, provider, or substrate is unavailable, keeping read-only readiness and audit visible while disabling dependent side-effect paths. Emitted readiness, error, and audit signals use the controlled, OpenTelemetry-informed observability vocabulary.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by the cited platform-services and service-catalog-registry runtime proofs (and the composed-provider readiness proof), demonstrating catalog aggregation and tenant-scoped readiness reporting as hermetic/runtime lineage rather than a current-readiness claim. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
